---
title: "Hands-on Exercise 4b"
author: "JIN QINHAO"
---

## **Getting Started**

### **Installing and launching R packages**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(ggstatsplot, tidyverse)
```
:::


### **Importing data**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Rows: 322 Columns: 7
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (4): ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE
dbl (3): ENGLISH, MATHS, SCIENCE

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```


:::
:::


## **One-sample test: *gghistostats()* method**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
set.seed(1234)

gghistostats(
  data = exam,
  x = ENGLISH,
  type = "bayes",
  test.value = 60,
  xlab = "English scores"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Two-sample mean test: *ggbetweenstats()***


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggbetweenstats(
  data = exam,
  x = GENDER, 
  y = MATHS,
  type = "np",
  messages = FALSE
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Oneway ANOVA Test: *ggbetweenstats()* method**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggbetweenstats(
  data = exam,
  x = RACE, 
  y = ENGLISH,
  type = "p",
  mean.ci = TRUE, 
  pairwise.comparisons = TRUE, 
  pairwise.display = "s",
  p.adjust.method = "fdr",
  messages = FALSE
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Significant Test of Correlation: *ggscatterstats()***


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggscatterstats(
  data = exam,
  x = MATHS,
  y = ENGLISH,
  marginal = FALSE,
  )
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Significant Test of Association (Depedence) : *ggbarstats()* methods**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam1 <- exam %>% 
  mutate(MATHS_bins = 
           cut(MATHS, 
               breaks = c(0,60,75,85,100))
)
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggbarstats(exam1, 
           x = MATHS_bins, 
           y = GENDER)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::
